revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大悟法久志]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1947|3|20}}|death_date={{death date|2011|8|29}}|birth_place={{ill|Nakatsu|qid=Q861221|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}|death_place={{ill|Usa|qid=Q114147|1=Usa, Ōita}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|baseball manager|qid=Q1186921|1=Manager (baseball)}}; {{ill|secondary school teacher|qid=Q5758653|1=secondary school teacher}}|given_name={{ill|Hisashi|qid=Q18510549|1=Hisashi}}}}
{{Infobox baseball player | name = Hisashi Daigobō (大悟法 久志) | team = | position = | number = | image = | image_size = | caption = | nationality = {{JPN|qq=}} | birth_place = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|qid=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu, Ōita|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} | birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1947|3|20|no|qq=}} | death_date = {{Death date and age|1947|3|20|2011|8|29|qq=}} | bats = | throws = | position_text = {{ill|Catcher|en|Catcher|qid=Q1050571|lt=Catcher|1=Catcher}} | teams = * {{ill|Asanuma Gakuen Nakatsu High School|ja|浅沼学園中津高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asanuma Gakuen Nakatsu High School|1=Asanuma Gakuen Nakatsu High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557128}} * {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|qid=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}} (Semi-hardball team) | management_title = Coaching history | management_teams = * {{ill|Yanagigaura High School|ja|柳ヶ浦高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagigaura High School|1=Yanagigaura High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11534061}} * {{ill|Meihō Junior and Senior High School|ja|明豊中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Junior and Senior High School|1=Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512637}} * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Seihō High School|ja|福岡県立青豊高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Seihō High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Seihou High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592602}} }} '''Hisashi Daigobō''' ({{lang-ja|大悟法 久志|qq=}}, March 20, 1947 – August 29, 2011<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.sponichi.co.jp/baseball/news/2011/08/31/kiji/K20110831001518360.html|title=Imamiya Shock: Passing of Coach Daigobō Who Led Meihō to Top 8|date=2011-08-31|newspaper=Sponichi Annex|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130630214147/http://www.sponichi.co.jp/baseball/news/2011/08/31/kiji/K20110831001518360.html|archivedate=2013-06-30|qq=}}</ref>) was a Japanese {{ill|high school baseball|ja|高校野球|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/high school baseball|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の高校野球|1=Kōshien baseball tournaments|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5353630}} coach from {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|qid=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu, Ōita|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}.<ref>''Kōshien High School Baseball Directory,'' Hiroshi Morioka, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2004, ISBN 978-4490106503.</ref> He coached teams at {{ill|Yanagigaura High School|ja|柳ヶ浦高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagigaura High School|1=Yanagigaura High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11534061}}, {{ill|Meihō Junior and Senior High School|ja|明豊中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Junior and Senior High School|1=Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512637}}, and {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Seihō High School|ja|福岡県立青豊高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Seihō High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Seihou High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592602}}. == Career == Daigobō graduated from {{ill|Asanuma Gakuen Nakatsu High School|ja|浅沼学園中津高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asanuma Gakuen Nakatsu High School|1=Asanuma Gakuen Nakatsu High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557128}} and {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|qid=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}, where he played as a {{ill|Catcher|en|Catcher|qid=Q1050571|lt=catcher|1=Catcher}} in the university's semi-hardball team and represented Japan in international competitions. In his senior year, he began coaching at {{ill|Yanagigaura High School|ja|柳ヶ浦高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagigaura High School|1=Yanagigaura High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11534061}}, eventually joining as a full-time teacher and head baseball coach in 1969. Despite the challenge of recruiting players at a newly co-educational school, his dedicated coaching and collaboration with {{ill|Shigemi Fujikubo|ja|藤久保茂己|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigemi Fujikubo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led the team to their first {{ill|Koshien|en|Koshien|qid=Q841854|lt=Kōshien|1=Koshien}} appearance in {{ill|1976 Summer Kōshien|ja|第58回全国高等学校野球選手権大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1976 Summer Kōshien|qq=}}. Daigobō achieved significant milestones, including his first victory at Kōshien in {{ill|1987 Summer Kōshien|ja|第69回全国高等学校野球選手権大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1987 Summer Kōshien|qq=}}, supported by standout player {{ill|Yūichi Kiyohara|ja|清原雄一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūichi Kiyohara|1=Yūichi Kiyohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11560526}}. His teams consistently performed well, reaching the semifinals in {{ill|1994 Summer Kōshien|ja|第76回全国高等学校野球選手権大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994 Summer Kōshien|qq=}}. In 1998, Daigobō transitioned to {{ill|Meihō High School|ja|明豊高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明豊中学校・高等学校|1=Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512637}} as general coach and later head coach, achieving his first Kōshien entry for the school in 2004. He led the team to multiple appearances, including a top-eight finish in {{ill|2009 Summer Kōshien|ja|第91回全国高等学校野球選手権大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2009 Summer Kōshien|qq=}}, with standout players such as {{ill|Kenta Imamiya|en|Kenta Imamiya|qid=Q197704|lt=Kenta Imamiya|1=Kenta Imamiya}}. He retired as coach in 2009. In 2010, Daigobō became head coach at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Seihō High School|ja|福岡県立青豊高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Seihō High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Seihou High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592602}}, continuing his commitment to high school baseball until his passing in 2011 from respiratory failure.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.47news.jp/sports/hibaseball/2011/09/post_20110912102556.html|title=Farewell Ceremony Held for Coach Daigobō|date=2011-09-12|newspaper=47NEWS|agency=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20111020153825/http://www.47news.jp/sports/hibaseball/2011/09/post_20110912102556.html|archivedate=2011-10-20|qq=}}</ref> == Legacy == * Daigobō's surname originates from {{ill|Daigobō Village|ja|大悟法村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigobō Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|qid=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province|1=Buzen Province}}, and his family historically served as {{ill|Shinkan (official)|en|Shinkan (official)|qid=Q11589454|lt=Shinto priests|1=Shinkan (official)}} for {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}}. * As an avid collector of antiques and a poet, he composed {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|qid=Q258331|lt=waka|1=Tanka}}, blending his passion for baseball and poetry. == Managerial Record == ; Yanagigaura High School * Spring: 1 appearance (1 win, 1 loss); Summer: 4 appearances (4 wins, 3 losses) ; Meihō High School * Spring: 2 appearances (1 win, 2 losses); Summer: 2 appearances (3 wins, 2 losses) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|大悟法久志|version=95764182|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:大悟法久志]] [[ja:大悟法 久志]]
